div1.entryFree:
Untaught (untaúght; before a subst. úntaught) 1) not communicated and imparted by instruction: royalty unlearned, honour u. Cymb. IV, 2, 178.
2) notinstructed, not accustomed: used to command, u. to plead for favour, H6B IV, 1, 122.
3) unmannerly: their u. love must needs appear offence, Meas. II, 4, 29. called them u. knaves, H4A I, 3, 43. O thou u., what manners is in this, Rom. V, 3, 214.
